value.found_quote: To define your own custom agents, add standalone TOML files under `~/.codex/agents/` for personal agents or `.codex/agents/` for project-scoped agents. Each file defines one custom agent. Codex loads these files as configuration layers for spawned sessions, so custom agents can override the same settings as a normal Codex session config.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes the ability to define custom agents via TOML files placed under ~/.codex/agents/ or .codex/agents/. These TOML files support different model configurations (e.g., `model`, `model_reasoning_effort`) and instructions (e.g., `developer_instructions`), directly substantiating the claim. The feature is described as currently available ("Current Codex releases enable subagent workflows by default"), making its lifecycle status active.
